business_administration_remaining_course.title: Business Law and Ethics
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Study of legal principles affecting business operations including contracts, torts, employment law, and corporate governance. Students learn to identify legal issues, understand ethical business practices, and make decisions that comply with legal and ethical standards.
learning_objectives: Understand fundamental legal principles affecting business
Analyze contract formation, performance, and breach issues
Evaluate ethical dilemmas in business decision-making
Apply employment and labor law principles
Assess corporate governance and compliance requirements
covered_materials: Legal system and sources of law

business_administration_remaining_course.title: E-commerce and Digital Business
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Comprehensive study of electronic commerce and digital business models including online marketing, digital payments, and technology-enabled business transformation. Students learn to develop and manage digital business strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand e-commerce business models and strategies
Develop online marketing and customer acquisition strategies
Evaluate digital payment systems and security measures
Analyze digital transformation and business process redesign
Assess the impact of emerging technologies on business
covered_materials: E-commerce business models and revenue streams
Online consumer behavior and digital marketing
Website design and user experience optimization
Supply chain management in digital commerce
Legal and regulatory issues in e-commerce

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Supply Chain Management
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Advanced study of supply chain design, management, and optimization including supplier relationships, logistics, and global supply chain strategies. Students learn to create efficient and resilient supply networks.
learning_objectives: Design efficient supply chain networks and processes
Develop supplier relationship management strategies
Apply logistics and distribution optimization techniques
Understand global supply chain challenges and solutions
Implement supply chain risk management practices
covered_materials: Supply chain risk management and resilience

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Entrepreneurship
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Exploration of entrepreneurial mindset, opportunity recognition, and new venture creation. Students learn to develop business ideas, create business plans, and understand the challenges and rewards of starting and growing new businesses.
learning_objectives: Develop entrepreneurial thinking and opportunity recognition skills
Create comprehensive business plans and financial projections
Understand funding sources and investment strategies
Evaluate market opportunities and competitive positioning
Apply lean startup methodologies and rapid prototyping
covered_materials: Marketing and sales for new ventures

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Leadership and Organizational Behavior
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Study of leadership theories, organizational behavior, and team dynamics in modern business environments. Students develop leadership skills and learn to manage organizational change and cultural transformation.
learning_objectives: Apply leadership theories and styles to various situations
Understand individual and group behavior in organizations
Develop effective team building and conflict resolution skills
Implement organizational change and culture transformation
Evaluate motivation theories and employee engagement strategies
covered_materials: Individual behavior and personality in organizations

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Investment and Portfolio Management
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Advanced study of investment analysis, portfolio theory, and asset management including security valuation, risk assessment, and portfolio optimization strategies for individual and institutional investors.
learning_objectives: Analyze various investment securities and their characteristics
Apply portfolio theory and modern portfolio management
Evaluate risk and return trade-offs in investment decisions
Understand market efficiency and behavioral finance concepts
Develop investment strategies for different investor profiles

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Corporate Strategy
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Advanced strategic management focusing on corporate-level strategy including mergers and acquisitions, diversification, and international expansion. Students learn to make complex strategic decisions in dynamic business environments.
learning_objectives: Develop corporate-level strategies and diversification plans
Evaluate mergers, acquisitions, and strategic alliances
Analyze international expansion and global strategy options
Understand corporate governance and stakeholder management
Apply strategic decision-making frameworks to complex problems

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Business Communication
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Development of professional communication skills for business contexts including written, oral, and digital communication. Students learn to communicate effectively with diverse audiences and stakeholders in various business situations.
learning_objectives: Write clear and persuasive business documents
Deliver effective presentations to business audiences
Apply intercultural communication skills in global business
Use digital communication tools and platforms effectively
Develop crisis communication and stakeholder engagement strategies

business_administration_remaining_course.title: Project Management Fundamentals
business_administration_remaining_course.description: Introduction to project management principles and practices for business applications. Students learn project planning, execution, and control while understanding project management tools and methodologies.
learning_objectives: Apply project management fundamentals to business projects
Develop project plans, schedules, and budgets
Understand project risk management and quality control
Use project management software and tools effectively
Evaluate project success and lessons learned
covered_materials: Project scope, time, and cost management
